xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfRegisteredOfficeOfCom: KUTTUKARAN CENTRE, MAMANGALAM, COCHIN, ERNAKULAM, KERALA, IN 682025
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfCorporateOfficeOfComp: KUTTUKARAN CENTRE, MAMANGALAM COCHIN,ERNAKULAM, KERALA, INDIA, 682025
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ProductOrServiceSoldByTheEntit: Wholesale and retail sale of new vehicles (passenger motor vehicles, ambulances, minibuses, jeeps, trucks,trailers and semi-trailers)
Wholesale and retail sale of used motor vehicles
Maintenance and repair of motor vehicles
Sale of motor vehicle parts and accessories
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ABriefOnTypesOfCustomersExplan: a New Vehicle Buyers b Periodic Maintenance c Vehicle Repair Services d Pre-owned Vehicles Customers e Driving Learners f Insurance Renewals
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RemarksStakeHolderGroupFromWho: No complaints or grievances were received from communities.
No complaints or grievances were received from communities
No complaints or grievances were received from investors.
No complaints or grievances were received from investors
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForGrievanceRedressPoli: https://www. popularmaruti.com/ investor-relations/ wp-content/ uploads/2025/03/ Investor_Grievance_ Redressal_Policy.pdf
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RemarksStakeHolderGroupFromWho: No complaints or grievance were received from the shareholders.
No complaints or grievance were received from employees & workers.
7% reported unjustified complaints which is reported to OEM Level and force closed
No complaints or grievances were received from value chain partners or any other stakeholders
No complaints or grievances were received from value chain partners or any other stakeholders
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Increasing regulatory pressure on vehicle emissions
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Develop and promote the sale of electric vehicles (EVs) and invest in emission control technologies
Invest in renewable energy sources for dealership operations and promote Evs
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Regulatory requirements for proper disposal of automotive waste
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Implement comprehensive waste management systems and recycling programs
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Increasing consumer preference for environmentally friendly vehicles
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Expand the EV product range and provide incentives for EV purchases
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Ensuring fair labour practices and inclusivity
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Develop policies and training programs to ensure compliance with labour laws and promote inclusivity.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Need to reduce operational costs and carbon footprint
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Implement energy efficient practices and technologies in dealership operations
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Building strong relationships with the local community
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Engage in corporate social responsibility (CSR) activities and community development projects
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Regulatory and community pressure to manage water resources sustainably
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Implement water saving technologies and practices in dealership operations
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Potential disruption to supply chain and operations due to climate events
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Develop and implement a climate resilience plan for operations and supply chain
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Focusing on growth through strategic expansion into new geographical markets. Both urban and rural regions offer untapped potential, enabling the company to serve a wider customer base and capitalize on regional demand trends.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Actively expanding the company’s physical presence across various regions and initiating operations in new markets. This includes setting up dealerships in high-potential areas, tailoring offerings to local preferences, and strengthening distribution and service networks.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Underutilization of digital tools and platforms and lack of awareness and understanding of digital tools among teams may hinder operational efficiency, reduce productivity, and limit the overall effectiveness of internal processes and customer engagement efforts.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Launch of a digital enablement program, including awareness sessions and workshops to improve digital literacy and embed digital thinking and practices
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheNationalAndInternatio: The company is a trusted distribution partner for leading automobile OEMs, and all products are manufactured at the OEMs’ own plants and facilities. These manufacturing operations adhere to stringent global quality standards such as ISO 9001 (Quality Management), IATF 16949 (Automotive Quality Management), and others. The OEMs also comply with additional certifications across operational areas including HR (e.g., SA 8000 for Social Accountability), occupational health and safety (e.g., ISO 45001), and environment management (e.g., ISO 14001), in alignment with the 9 principles of the National Guidelines on Responsible Business Conduct (NGRBC). As of now, the company itself does not hold independent product or process certifications, since all quality controls are managed by OEMs at their own certified plants. However, going forward, the company may consider adopting globally recognized certifications and standards, depending on regulatory requirements and strategic alignment.

The company is a trusted distribution partner for leading automobile OEMs, and all products are manufactured at the OEMs’ own plants and facilities. These manufacturing operations adhere to stringent global quality standards such as ISO 9001 (Quality Management), IATF 16949 (Automotive Quality Management), and others. The OEMs also comply with additional certifications across operational areas including HR (e.g., SA 8000 for Social Accountability), occupational health and safety (e.g., ISO 45001), and environment management (e.g., ISO 14001), in alignment with the 9 principles of the National Guidelines on Responsible Business Conduct (NGRBC). As of now, the company itself does not hold independent product or process certifications, since all quality controls are managed by OEMs at their own certified plants. However, going forward, the company may consider adopting globally recognized certifications and standards, depending on regulatory requirements and strategic alignment.

The company is a trusted distribution partner for leading automobile OEMs, and all products are manufactured at the OEMs’ own plants and facilities. These manufacturing operations adhere to stringent global quality standards such as ISO 9001 (Quality Management), IATF 16949 (Automotive Quality Management), and others. The OEMs also comply with additional certifications across operational areas including HR (e.g., SA 8000 for Social Accountability), occupational health and safety (e.g., ISO 45001), and environment management (e.g., ISO 14001), in alignment with the 9 principles of the National Guidelines on Responsible Business Conduct (NGRBC). As of now, the company itself does not hold independent product or process certifications, since all quality controls are managed by OEMs at their own certified plants. However, going forward, the company may consider adopting globally recognized certifications and standards, depending on regulatory requirements and strategic alignment.

The company is a trusted distribution partner for leading automobile OEMs, and all products are manufactured at the OEMs’ own plants and facilities. These manufacturing operations adhere to stringent global quality standards such as ISO 9001 (Quality Management), IATF 16949 (Automotive Quality Management), and others. The OEMs also comply with additional certifications across operational areas including HR (e.g., SA 8000 for Social Accountability), occupational health and safety (e.g., ISO 45001), and environment management (e.g., ISO 14001), in alignment with the 9 principles of the National Guidelines on Responsible Business Conduct (NGRBC). As of now, the company itself does not hold independent product or process certifications, since all quality controls are managed by OEMs at their own certified plants. However, going forward, the company may consider adopting globally recognized certifications and standards, depending on regulatory requirements and strategic alignment.

The company is a trusted distribution partner for leading automobile OEMs, and all products are manufactured at the OEMs’ own plants and facilities. These manufacturing operations adhere to stringent global quality standards such as ISO 9001 (Quality Management), IATF 16949 (Automotive Quality Management), and others. The OEMs also comply with additional certifications across operational areas including HR (e.g., SA 8000 for Social Accountability), occupational health and safety (e.g., ISO 45001), and environment management (e.g., ISO 14001), in alignment with the 9 principles of the National Guidelines on Responsible Business Conduct (NGRBC). As of now, the company itself does not hold independent product or process certifications, since all quality controls are managed by OEMs at their own certified plants. However, going forward, the company may consider adopting globally recognized certifications and standards, depending on regulatory requirements and strategic alignment.

These manufacturing operations adhere to stringent global quality standards such as ISO 9001 (Quality Management), IATF 16949 (Automotive Quality Management), and others. The OEMs also comply with additional certifications across operational areas including HR (e.g., SA 8000 for Social Accountability), occupational health and safety (e.g., ISO 45001), and environment management (e.g., ISO 14001), in alignment with the 9 principles of the National Guidelines on Responsible Business Conduct (NGRBC). As of now, the company itself does not hold independent product or process certifications, since all quality controls are managed by OEMs at their own certified plants. However, going forward, the company may consider adopting globally recognized certifications and standards, depending on regulatory requirements and strategic alignment.

These manufacturing operations adhere to stringent global quality standards such as ISO 9001 (Quality Management), IATF 16949 (Automotive Quality Management), and others. The company is a trusted distribution partner for leading automobile OEMs, and all products are manufactured at the OEMs’ own plants and facilities. These manufacturing operations adhere to stringent global quality standards such as ISO 9001 (Quality Management), IATF 16949 (Automotive Quality Management), and others. The OEMs also comply with additional certifications across operational areas including HR (e.g., SA 8000 for Social Accountability), occupational health and safety (e.g., ISO 45001), and environment management (e.g., ISO 14001), in alignment with the 9 principles of the National Guidelines on Responsible Business Conduct (NGRBC). As of now, the company itself does not hold independent product or process certifications, since all quality controls are managed by OEMs at their own certified plants. However, going forward, the company may consider adopting globally recognized certifications and standards, depending on regulatory requirements and strategic alignment.

The company is a trusted distribution partner for leading automobile OEMs, and all products are manufactured at the OEMs’ own plants and facilities. These manufacturing operations adhere to stringent global quality standards such as ISO 9001 (Quality Management), IATF 16949 (Automotive Quality Management), and others. The OEMs also comply with additional certifications across operational areas including HR (e.g., SA 8000 for Social Accountability), occupational health and safety (e.g., ISO 45001), and environment management (e.g., ISO 14001), in alignment with the 9 principles of the National Guidelines on Responsible Business Conduct (NGRBC). xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StatementByDirectorResponsible: Refer to Chairperson's and Managing Director's message in Annual Report.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfSpecifiedCommitteeOfT: Corporate Social Responsibility Committee and Stakeholders’ Relationship Committee addresses key aspects of the sustainability related issues.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescriptionOfOtherFrequencyFor: The process is reveiwed from time to time, without adhering to a regular frequency
The process is reveiwed from time to time, without adhering to a regular frequency

Our adherence to statutory principles, requirements, and other applicable laws ensures that the company’s management conducts meetings based on the requirements. These meetings are scheduled as necessary, rather than on a fixed yearly, half-yearly, or other periodic basis.

Our adherence to statutory principles, requirements, and other applicable laws ensures that the company’s management conducts meetings based on the requirements. These meetings are scheduled as necessary, rather than on a fixed yearly, half-yearly, or other periodic basis.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AnyOtherReasonsIfPoliciesNotCo: No specific targets have been set.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TopicsOrPrinciplesCoveredUnder: 1 LODR compliances, Insider Trading compliances, Board and Committee responsibilities, 2 Enterprise Risk Management (ERM Workshop) 3 Environment Social and Governance (ESG Programme)
1 LODR compliances, Insider Trading compliances, Board and Committee responsibilities, 2 Enterprise Risk Management (ERM Workshop) 3 Environment Social and Governance (ESG Programme)

Management Development Program for Driving Success: Building Customer - Centric Excellence and Resilience for leadership Team from IIM Kozhikode, Team Management, Time Management, Outbound Training Program on Synergy and Collaboration, Customer Excellence, Finance for Non-Finance, AI/ ChatGPT for Managers, Sales Excellence training by Mercuri International for Branch Head, Transactional Analysis and Empathy Trainings for Works Managers and Assistant Works Managers, Design Thinking, Train The Trainer, Specialized EV training for trainers, Customer Excellence and Centric Trainings, Management Trainee Induction and Orientation Trainings, New Joiners Induction Trainings

Transactional analysis and Empathy, Communication Skills, Listening Skills, Customer-Centric Approach, Customer Excellence Training, Time Management, Excel Training, Sales/Service Process Training, Refresher Training on Product, Process, SOP, Technology, Applications, New Joiner Induction Training, Company Policy and HR Induction, Incentive/Offer Trainings, Sales Pitch and Mastery, Objection Handling, Upselling and Cross Selling Session for sales and service, Telecaller/ Telemarketing Trainings
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:BriefOfTheMonetaryCaseForPenal: The Chief Judicial Magistrate, Thiruvallur, has issued summons in CC 764/2023 against Mr. John K. Paul (Manager and Occupier) to answer charges related to the offences punishable under Section 6(1) Rule 12-B (3) and (4), Section 31 Rule 56-B (1) (2) (3) and (4) of the Factories Act, 1948. The charges are based on a complaint submitted by the Assistant Director of the Industrial Safety and Health Department, Thiruvallur- which arises from an inspection and a show cause notice dated 22-08-2023, issued by the complainant, calling upon Mr.John K. Paul (being the Occupier and Manager of the premises) to explain why he should not be prosecuted for various irregularities observed during the inspection of the Noombal Sales PDI Yard premises of the Company, Popular Vehicles and Services Limited,of which Mr. John K. Paul was the Director.

The Chief Judicial Magistrate, Thiruvallur, has issued summons in CC 765/2023 against Mr. John K. Paul (Manager and Occupier) to answer charges related to the offences punishable under Section 41 Rule 61-O and Section 61 (1) (9) Rule 79 of the Factories Act,1948. Mr. John K Paul appeared before the Chief Judicial Magistrate, Thiruvallur, and upon reading the charges, he pleaded guilty. Recording the plea, the case was disposed off on 09th October, 2024 imposing to pay a fine amount of Rs. 50,000 as punishment.

The Chief Judicial Magistrate, Thiruvallur, has issued summons STC 6037/2023 against Mr. John K. Paul (Manager and Occupier) to answer charges related to the offences punishable under Section 4(1) (b) (i) of Labour Laws (Simplification of Procedure for Furnishing Returns and Maintaining Registers by Certain Establishments) Act, 1988. The charges are based on a complaint submitted by the Assistant Director of the Industrial Safety and Health Department, Thiruvallur which arises from an inspection of the Noombal Sales PDI Yard premises of the Company, Popular Vehicles and Services Limited, of which Mr. John K. Paul was the Director; whereby various Labour Law non-compliances were found like the failure to maintain the register of persons employed cum employment card in Form I and the Muster Roll cum wage register in Form III of the Labour Laws Act. Mr. John K. Paul appeared before the Chief Judicial Magistrate, Thiruvallur and upon reading the charges, he pleaded guilty. Recording the plea, the case was disposed of on 09/10/2024.
Mr. John K Paul appeared before the Chief Judicial Magistrate, Thiruvallur, and upon reading the charges, he pleaded guilty. Recording the plea, the case was disposed off on 09th October, 2024 imposing to pay a fine amount of Rs. 2,000 as punishment.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:BriefOfTheMonetaryCaseForSettl:
During a routine inspection on 18th January 2014 at the Company’s Vadakara Service Centre, Kozhikode, the Assistant Labour Officer (ALO) Vadakara found that the Company has paid lesser wages than the wages fixed under the Minimum Wages Act 1948, in the respective categories to 14 employees of the Service Centre.
The MCP No12/2014 was finally disposed off vide order dated 17th August, 2023, by the Deputy Labour Commissioner, Kozhikode, directing PVSL to pay a total amount of Rs. 2,33,690 being the Minimum Wages arrears under section 20(3) of the Minimum Wages Act, 1948.

During a routine inspection on 15th September 2018 at the Company’s Kozhikode Service Centre, the Assistant Labour Officer of Kozhikode 2nd Circle, found that the Company has paid lesser wages than the wages fixed under the Minimum Wages Act 1948, in the respective categories to 12 employees of the Service Centre.
The MCP No36/2019 was finally disposed off vide order dated 26th August, 2023, by the Deputy Labour Commissioner, Kozhikode, directing PVSL to pay a total amount of Rs. 1,02,614 being the Minimum Wages arrears under section 20(3) of the Minimum Wages Act, 1948.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesInPlaceToS: Popular Vehicles & Services Limited does not have any processes in place to reclaim products for reusing, recycling, or disposal at end-of-life for plastics, e-waste, hazardous waste, or other waste, as the generation of such waste is either nil or negligible due to the nature of its operations. The Company does not engage in manufacturing or packaging activities, and hence there is no plastic waste generated. E-waste, if any, is minimal and pertains to end-of-life electronic equipment used internally, all of which is responsibly disposed of through authorised e-waste collectors. No hazardous waste is generated, as the Company does not manufacture or trade in products that produce such waste. Other routine waste is managed in accordance with applicable municipal guidelines.

Popular Vehicles & Services Limited does not have any processes in place to reclaim products for reusing, recycling, or disposal at end-of-life for plastics, e-waste, hazardous waste, or other waste, as the generation of such waste is either nil or negligible due to the nature of its operations. The Company does not engage in manufacturing or packaging activities, and hence there is no plastic waste generated. E-waste, if any, is minimal and pertains to end-of-life electronic equipment used internally, all of which is responsibly disposed of through authorised e-waste collectors. No hazardous waste is generated, as the Company does not manufacture or trade in products that produce such waste. Other routine waste is managed in accordance with applicable municipal guidelines.

Popular Vehicles & Services Limited does not have any processes in place to reclaim products for reusing, recycling, or disposal at end-of-life for plastics, e-waste, hazardous waste, or other waste, as the generation of such waste is either nil or negligible due to the nature of its operations. The Company does not engage in manufacturing or packaging activities, and hence there is no plastic waste generated. E-waste, if any, is minimal and pertains to end-of-life electronic equipment used internally, all of which is responsibly disposed of through authorised e-waste collectors. No hazardous waste is generated, as the Company does not manufacture or trade in products that produce such waste. Other routine waste is managed in accordance with applicable municipal guidelines.

Popular Vehicles & Services Limited does not have any processes in place to reclaim products for reusing, recycling, or disposal at end-of-life for plastics, e-waste, hazardous waste, or other waste, as the generation of such waste is either nil or negligible due to the nature of its operations. The Company does not engage in manufacturing or packaging activities, and hence there is no plastic waste generated. E-waste, if any, is minimal and pertains to end-of-life electronic equipment used internally, all of which is responsibly disposed of through authorised e-waste collectors. No hazardous waste is generated, as the Company does not manufacture or trade in products that produce such waste. Other routine waste is managed in accordance with applicable municipal guidelines.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WhetherAnyStepsAreBeingTakenBy: Not all our premises are accessible for differently abled.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkOfEqualOppertunityPolic:
We have an Equal Opportunity, Diversity, and Inclusion Policy. All employees are encouraged to report any concerns or incidents of discrimination and harassment to their respective HR SPOC.
https://www.popularmaruti.com/investor-relations/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/Equal-Oppurtunity-Diversity-and-includion-Policy-PVSL.pdf
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfMechanismAvailableToR: In the event of a work-related grievance, our permanent workers can first raise it with their respective reporting managers, who will initiate the redressal process. If the concern or complaint is against the reporting manager, the employee can escalate it to the next level manager. The employee's HR SPOC will also partner in the redressal process.

In the event of a work-related grievance,all our workers can first raise it with their respective reporting managers, who will initiate the redressal process. If the concern or complaint is against the reporting manager, the employee can escalate it to the next level manager. The employee's HR SPOC will also partner in the redressal process.

In the event of a work-related grievance, our permanent employees can first raise it with their respective reporting managers, who will initiate the redressal process. If the concern or complaint is against the reporting manager, the employee can escalate it to the next level manager. The employee's HR SPOC will also partner in the redressal process.

In the event of a work-related grievance, all our employees can first raise it with their respective reporting managers, who will initiate the redressal process. If the concern or complaint is against the reporting manager, the employee can escalate it to the next level manager. The employee's HR SPOC will also partner in the redressal process.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOccupationalHealthAnd: We have implemented a new Occupational Health and Safety Policy. This policy applies to all employees, contractors, and visitors within our premises, including sales areas, service workshops, and parking lots.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DesclosureOfTheProcessesUsedTo: 1 Conduct regular safety inspections and address identified hazards. 2 Regularly inspect the premises for potential hazards, vehicles and equipments for safety compliance and rectify any identified risks.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMeasuresTakenByTheE: To ensure a safe and healthy workplace, we conduct regular inspections and maintenance by certified authorities, implement robust safety protocols, provide comprehensive training, and conduct emergency response drills and first aid training.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RemarksforComplaintsExplanator: Currently, we have no mechanism to record the number of safety and health complaints made by employees and workers. However, we are in the process of implementing a system to address this. Any such complaints received have been addressed and resolved at the local branch level.

Currently, we have no mechanism to record the number of safety and health complaints made by employees and workers. However, we are in the process of implementing a system to address this. Any such complaints received have been addressed and resolved at the local branch level.

Currently, we have no mechanism to record the number of safety and health complaints made by employees and workers. However, we are in the process of implementing a system to address this. Any such complaints received have been addressed and resolved at the local branch level.

Currently, we have no mechanism to record the number of safety and health complaints made by employees and workers. However, we are in the process of implementing a system to address this. Any such complaints received have been addressed and resolved at the local branch level.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesForIdentif: Popular Vehicles and Services Limited (PVSL) recognizes stakeholders as valued partners in its mission for long term value creation. Effective stakeholder involvement is crucial for achieving the company’s strategic goals. PVSL has recognized both internal and external stakeholders. At present, the stakeholders who significantly influence the Company’s operations and functioning comprises employees, shareholders, customers, vendors, insurance companies, OEM, local communities, government and regulators and lenders.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Stock exchange disclosures Website AGMs, public and media announcements Annual Report Quarterly investor calls Dedicated external Investor Relations team.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Shareholders are fundamental to business growth and development

Being into automobile sector, the road culture and safe road habits helps in saving lives and livelihood of the communities around. Partnering with schools, we have strived to inculcate culture of awareness among the present and future drivers.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Compliance with all applicable laws. Representation through Industry bodies. Participation in Government consultation programmes
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Compliance with applicable laws and regulations help maintain a positive relation with Government and is essential to drive important policies in the sector.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Real time engagement Email HR application (Voyonfolks) Notice board Daily meeting and briefings. Townhall meetings Timely internal communications Whatsapp Group
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: At PVSL employees form the core of the business and service. Engagements with them help to analyze working environment, career enhancement, health, safety and engagement activities.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Direct feedback Social media handles Website, E-mail Periodic market research.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: PVSL has been the choice for customers. Customer feedback helps the company improve its services.
Vendors are integral part and their services influence the business operations and quality of customers experience.
PVSL offers insurance renewal services and positive relationship with insurance companies ensure the quality, efficiency, reliability of the services.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: E-mail Telephone Meetings Dealer Management System.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Being franchise model, the OEM interactions/ relations/ communications is integral to the success of the business.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Meetings Sharing regular updates on financial performance. Ongoing communication and relationship
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Caters to the financial assistance for the growth and diversification of the company.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheInternalMechanismsI: Implemented Human Right Policy. We encourage all employees to report any concerns, complaints, or grievances related to workplace issues, including but not limited to discrimination, harassment, unethical behavior, or violation of policies. All reports will be taken seriously and handled promptly, confidentially, and impartially.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MechanismsToPreventAdverseCons: The organisation is equipped to handle discrimination and harassment cases by ensuring confidentiality, enforcing strict anti-retaliation measures, providing support systems like counselling, and conducting regular follow-ups. These mechanisms are in place to protect the complainant and maintain a fair and safe working environment if such cases arise.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionsT: Though not conducting specific assessments, the company regularly monitors workplace practices to prevent issues related to child labor,forced labor, sexual harassment, discrimination, and wage discrepancies. The company ensures that wages are paid according to agreements and legal requirements and has established rules and policies in these areas where mandatorily applicable.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfWasteManagementPracti: The company is committed to responsible waste management practices, ensuring that all hazardous and non-hazardous wastes are properly disposed of in accordance with environmental regulations. We prioritize reducing the use of hazardous and toxic chemicals in our processes and have implemented effective practices to manage such wastes. Our strategy includes minimizing waste generation, promoting recycling, and adopting safe disposal methods to protect the environment and support sustainability in our operations.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsToReceive: Community members can approach our staff or senior managers at any of our locations to raise and resolve grievances. Additionally, they can contact us through email or phone for any concerns or issues. These mechanisms ensure that we remain accessible and responsive to the needs of the community, fostering open communication and effective grievance redressal
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsInPlaceTo: Website - www.popularmaruti.com Inbound Toll Free - 1800 123 8090 OutBound - Post Booking Follow-up , Post Sales Follow-up, Post Service Follow-up Social Media - Facebook, Instagram Nodal - 9846298607 , Nodal@popularv.com Branch Walkins
